title: The Starlight Festival
content: The Starlight Festival is Springtown's most cherished annual event, held every summer solstice. Founded over fifty years ago by Mio's grandmother, the festival celebrates community and hope through the release of handmade paper lanterns into the night sky. Each lantern is crafted by townspeople and carries a personal wish. The festival features food stalls, live performances, fireworks designed by local inventor Hiro, and the signature lantern release at midnight. The festival grounds stretch from the cherry blossom park to the riverbank. Mio, the current organizer, inherited her grandmother's lantern patterns and sketchbooks, continuing the tradition of handcrafting each lantern with care.
